Patient Resources

Healthcare that puts patients first

Healthcare starts with you at CMC. You get a skilled team of caring professionals committed to improving your whole health.

Medical: (209) 636-5400 Dental: (209) 636-5555
Coverage

Health Coverage Assistance

CMC serves all patients regardless of their ability to pay. Our staff can help you enroll in coverage — no appointment needed.

Please bring your insurance ID card and any required copayments to your appointment.

Need enrollment help?
(209) 373-2824 — Certified Enrollment Counselors available

Insurance Accepted

  • ✓ Medi-Cal
  • ✓ Medi-Cal Managed Care (Health Plan of San Joaquin, Health Net, Partnership Health Plan of California)
  • ✓ Medicare
  • ✓ Medicare Advantage Plans
  • ✓ Covered California

Uninsured? Depending on your family size, income, and housing, you may qualify for free or low-cost care.

Health Records

Patient Portal

Access your health information anytime through our secure online portal.

  • ✓ Send and receive messages with your primary care provider
  • ✓ Access your health records
  • ✓ Request appointments
  • ✓ View latest test results
  • ✓ View, download, and share your personal health records
  • ✓ Search health and wellness information
Access Patient Portal

Setting Up Your Account

  1. Download the free NextGen Patient Portal app
  2. Visit nextmd.com to create your account
  3. Need your enrollment code? Call (209) 430-6222, Mon–Fri 8am–5pm

Medical Records Requests

Submit a completed form by:

  • Fax: (209) 762-6808
  • Email: prforms@cmcenters.org
  • Mail: P.O. Box 779, Stockton CA 95201

Health Information Dept: (209) 546-3870

Forms

Patient Forms

All forms are available in English and Spanish. Download, complete, and bring to your appointment or submit as instructed.

Patient Registration

English Spanish

Advance Directive

English Spanish

Medicare Annual Wellness Visit Health Risk Assessment

English Spanish

Medical Records Request

English Spanish

Sliding Fee Discount Application

English Spanish

Patient Complaint Form

Open Form
Your Rights

Patient Rights & Responsibilities

Your Rights

  • · Be treated with dignity and respect
  • · Fair treatment without discrimination of any kind
  • · Make informed decisions regarding your health care
  • · Refuse treatment and be informed of the consequences
  • · Know the names and roles of all caregivers
  • · Receive information in a language you understand
  • · Access your medical records
  • · Confidentiality of records and communications
  • · Choose another healthcare provider
  • · Receive an explanation of your bill
  • · Appropriate assessment and management of pain
  • · Receive care in a safe environment free from abuse or coercion
  • · Voice concerns and complaints
  • · Give or withhold informed consent

Your Responsibilities

  • · Provide accurate and complete information about your health
  • · Actively participate in decisions regarding your health care
  • · Keep scheduled appointments or call to reschedule
  • · Ask questions when something is unclear
  • · Follow the treatment plan agreed upon with your provider
  • · Treat all staff and other patients with courtesy and respect
  • · Respect the rights, privacy, and property of others
  • · Promptly meet any financial obligation agreed to with CMC

Patient Privacy

We value the privacy of every patient. You have rights regarding how your medical information is used and disclosed.

View Privacy Notice →
Billing

Payments & Billing

Our healthcare professionals see all patients, regardless of their ability to pay. Please bring your current insurance or member ID card to every appointment.

Depending on your insurance, payment may be required at time of service or billed monthly.

Accepted: Cash · Check · Visa · Mastercard · Discover · American Express

Billing Department:
(209) 636-5550

Good Faith Estimate

You have the right to receive a written cost estimate at least 1 business day before any non-emergency scheduled service. This covers medical items, tests, drugs, equipment, and facility fees.

You may dispute a bill that is $400 or more higher than the estimate you received.

More info: cms.gov/nosurprises or call (209) 636-5550

Our Approach

Patient-Centered Medical Home

CMC is certified as a Patient-Centered Medical Home (PCMH), meaning we take a team-based approach that puts patients at the center of care. Your care team includes your primary care provider alongside medical, dental, behavioral health professionals, specialty care, and assistance programs — all working together for your health.

Find a Provider Find a Location
CMC Centers

Providing quality, compassionate healthcare to all patients regardless of their ability to pay since 1968.
